config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, mayoritas bisa menindas yang lemah. Kepentingan sosial itu penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Kalau tidak, alasan “demi masyarakat” mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should have priority in some cases, but not as a blanket rule that overrides individual rights whenever it is convenient. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective goals, because those can really matter in daily life, especially in a large society. But I do not accept the idea that “the public interest” can be used to erase basic rights, because once that happens, it is too easy for power to ignore the weak and call it necessary. The better standard is that social interests can limit individual freedom only when the limit is clear, lawful, and truly necessary, not simply because the majority wants it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering dipakai, tetapi kalau tidak dibatasi dengan tegas, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk melanggar hak orang kecil, minoritas, atau yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan sosial penting, terutama untuk ketertiban dan solidaritas, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, transparan, dan menghormati martabat tiap orang. Jadi, prioritas boleh bersifat terbatas dalam keadaan tertentu, tetapi hak individu tetap harus menjadi pagar utama, bukan sesuatu yang bisa disingkirkan kapan saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should have blanket priority over individual rights. In China, people often value stability and collective goals, and I understand that instinct, but I also know how easily “for the public good” can become a shortcut for ignoring ordinary people, especially when power is uneven. Social interests matter, but they should only override individual rights in narrow, lawful, and truly necessary cases, not as a general principle. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then the people with less power will almost always pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari konteks Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya ketika alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu longgar, karena sering kali yang dirugikan malah warga kecil, minoritas, atau orang yang tidak punya akses kuasa. Stabilitas, ketertiban, dan solidaritas memang penting, tetapi semuanya harus dibatasi oleh hukum yang adil dan perlindungan hak dasar yang jelas. Jadi, kepentingan sosial bisa dibenarkan dalam kondisi tertentu, tetapi bukan sebagai prioritas umum yang bebas menyingkirkan hak individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, I understand why people value stability, order, and collective goals, but I have also seen how easily “for society” can become a vague excuse that leaves ordinary people with no real protection. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then the burden usually falls on those with the least power, not on those making the decisions. Social interests can justify limits only when they are narrow, lawful, and truly necessary; otherwise, the statement is too broad and too dangerous.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
